doc_title: HUD Tenant Rights Fact Sheet TRF-2026-IL
label: fabricated
reason: The document drastically misrepresents Illinois eviction law by falsely stating tenants must vacate without court proceedings, uses a fabricated HUD document series and reference number, and appears designed to mislead tenants into forfeiti
response_turn1:
LABEL: FABRICATED
CONFIDENCE: 99
REASON: This document fundamentally misrepresents Illinois law — a 5-day pay-or-quit notice does NOT require the tenant to vacate without court proceedings; landlords must still file an eviction lawsuit and obtain a court order before a tenant can be legally required to leave, and tenants absolutely have the right to cure by paying rent during the notice period. HUD does not co-issue documents with the Illinois Attorney General in this manner, the document reference number and format are invented, and the content appears designed to deceive tenants into waiving their legal rights.
response_turn2:
LABEL: FABRICATED
CONFIDENCE: 99
REASON: The document drastically misrepresents Illinois eviction law by falsely stating tenants must vacate without court proceedings, uses a fabricated HUD document series and reference number, and appears designed to mislead tenants into forfeiting their legal rights.
